mnemonics: Think of the exam like a final PROJECT release: Plan Review, Organize Goals, Justify Estimates, Evaluate Trade-offs, Communicate Thoroughly, Test Everything
keyPoints: The exam tests comprehensive understanding of software engineering principles and project management methodologies.
Key areas include requirements analysis, design patterns, development models (Agile, Waterfall), risk management, and quality assurance.
Questions often require applying theoretical knowledge to solve practical, scenario-based problems.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on coding and neglecting project management aspects (scheduling, budgeting, risk).
Not reading scenario-based questions carefully and missing key details or constraints.
Providing vague answers instead of using specific software engineering terminology and examples.
